system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:

{

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) is inside-out

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_Cmodels/runs/squareBend/3/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 51.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 100.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: Block hex is "inside-out"

**Error Location**: `system/blockMeshDict`, blocks — specifically Block 0: `hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)`

### Diagnosis

The vertex ordering in several hex blocks does not follow the right-hand rule convention required by OpenFOAM's `blockMesh`. When viewed from outside the block, the vertices should go counterclockwise around the bottom face and clockwise around the top face (or vice versa, depending on the winding convention).

Looking at **Block 0**: `hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` where:
- Bottom face: `(0 4 5 1)` = `(-50,25,25)`, `(0,25,-25)`, `(0,-25,-25)`, `(-50,-25,25)`
- Top face: `(3 7 6 2)` = `(-50,-25,25)`, `(0,-25,25)`, `(0,-25,-25)`, `(-50,25,-25)`

This is indeed inside-out because the normal direction doesn't point outward consistently.

**All four blocks need their vertex ordering corrected.** Additionally, looking more carefully:

- **Block 1**: `hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)` — check if consistent
- **Block 2**: `hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2)` — check consistency
- **Block 3**: `hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14)` — check consistency

Each block needs to have its 8 vertices properly reordered so that the block follows the standard OpenFOAM hex convention.

### Solution

Redefine each hex block with correct vertex ordering following the right-hand rule. For example, **Block 0** could be rewritten as:

```
hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)
```

Similarly, reorder all blocks. A recommended approach is to visualize the block corners and ensure that when walking around the bottom face, the interior of the block is always to your left (right-hand rule). Alternatively, refer to the official squareBend tutorial's `blockMeshDict` for the correct vertex ordering.

---

## Error 2: Cannot find "points" file

**Error Location**: `log.rhoSimpleFoam`

### Diagnosis

This is a cascading error. Because `blockMesh` failed (Error 1 above), no mesh was generated. Without a valid mesh, the solver cannot find the `constant/polyMesh/` directory containing `points`, `faces`, `cells`, etc., so `rhoSimpleFoam` naturally fails.

### Solution

Fix the `blockMeshDict` vertex orderings as described above, then re-run `blockMesh` followed by `rhoSimpleFoam`.

---

## Additional Issue: Boundary Name Mismatches

After fixing the blockMeshDict, there may also be mismatched boundary patches between `blockMeshDict` and the `0/` field files. The current `blockMeshDict` defines boundaries named `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, but the `0/` field files define `inlet`, `outlet`, `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`. After `blockMesh` succeeds, rename the boundary patches in the `0/` files to match those in `blockMeshDict`:

- Rename `inlet` → `innerWall`
- Rename `outlet` → `outerWall`
- Keep `lowerWall` and `upperWall` as-is

Alternatively, rename the patches in `blockMeshDict` to match the existing field files (`inlet`, `outlet`), whichever is easier after the block definitions are corrected.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Fix Block 0 vertex ordering from hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) to hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7); Fix Block 1 vertex ordering from hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2) to hex (7 6 10 9 3 2 11 8); Fix Block 2 vertex ordering from hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2) to hex (11 10 14 13 2 3 15 12); Fix Block 3 vertex ordering from hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) to hex (12 13 19 17 14 15 18 16)'}, {'file': '0/T', 'changes': 'Rename boundary inlet to innerWall; Rename boundary outlet to outerWall'}, {'file': '0/U', 'changes': 'Rename boundary inlet to innerWall; Rename boundary outlet to outerWall'}, {'file': '0/k', 'changes': 'Rename boundary inlet to innerWall; Rename boundary outlet to outerWall'}, {'file': '0/epsilon', 'changes': 'Rename boundary inlet to innerWall; Rename boundary outlet to outerWall'}, {'file': '0/nut', 'changes': 'Rename boundary inlet to innerWall; Rename boundary outlet to outerWall'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
